title: Email Pages, ResumeFit & Auth Accessibility
description: Fix accessibility issues in email verification pages and auth-adjacent components: add aria-hidden to decorative status icons in VerifyEmailConfirm and VerifyEmailPending, replace ResumeFitSection hardcoded amber color with design token, promote ResumeFitSection section header from non-semantic span to h3, add aria-hidden to HeroSection decorative SVG illustrations, ensure LandingPage HeroSection images have appropriate alt text, and add aria-label to auth provider social login buttons.

userStories.title: Add aria-hidden to VerifyEmailConfirm decorative status icons
userStories.description: VerifyEmailConfirm.jsx renders three decorative status icons — CheckCircle (~line 28) for success, XCircle (~lines 40 and 59) for error, and Loader (~line 18) for the loading state. These icons are decorative because the surrounding text already conveys the meaning ('Email verified', 'Verification failed', etc.). Add aria-hidden='true' to all three icon imports wherever they are rendered in this component.
acceptanceCriteria: VerifyEmailConfirm.jsx CheckCircle icon has aria-hidden='true'
VerifyEmailConfirm.jsx XCircle icon has aria-hidden='true'
VerifyEmailConfirm.jsx Loader icon has aria-hidden='true'
Build passes with no errors

userStories.title: Add aria-hidden to VerifyEmailPending Mail icon
userStories.description: VerifyEmailPending.jsx renders a Mail icon (~line 84) that is decorative — the heading and body text communicate the verification pending state. Add aria-hidden='true' to the Mail icon so screen readers skip it.
acceptanceCriteria: VerifyEmailPending.jsx Mail icon has aria-hidden='true'
Build passes with no errors

userStories.title: Replace ResumeFitSection hardcoded amber color with design token
userStories.description: ResumeFitSection.jsx uses hardcoded text-amber-600 dark:text-amber-400 (~line 21) for a score or status indicator. This bypasses the design token system and doesn't adapt to theme changes. Replace with the appropriate semantic token (e.g., text-warning or equivalent from the project's tailwind config).
acceptanceCriteria: ResumeFitSection.jsx does not use hardcoded amber-* Tailwind classes
Colors use semantic design tokens from the project theme
Build passes with no errors

userStories.title: Promote ResumeFitSection section header to semantic heading
userStories.description: ResumeFitSection.jsx renders a 'Resume Fit' section header as a non-semantic span (~lines 20 and 34) rather than a proper heading element. Screen reader users navigating by headings cannot find this section. Replace the span with an appropriate heading element (h3 or h4) and preserve visual styling via className.
acceptanceCriteria: ResumeFitSection.jsx 'Resume Fit' header uses a semantic heading element (h3 or h4)
Visual styling is preserved via className
Build passes with no errors

userStories.title: Audit and fix HeroSection decorative image and icon accessibility
userStories.description: HeroSection.jsx (LandingPage hero section component) likely renders decorative SVG illustrations, background graphics, or feature icons that convey visual appeal rather than information. Read HeroSection.jsx and add aria-hidden='true' to any purely decorative SVG or img elements. Any img that conveys meaningful content should have a descriptive alt attribute; purely decorative images should have alt='' or aria-hidden='true'.
acceptanceCriteria: HeroSection.jsx decorative SVG/img elements have aria-hidden='true' or alt=''
Informational images have descriptive alt text
Build passes with no errors

userStories.title: Add accessible names to GithubAuthButton and GoogleAuthButton
userStories.description: GithubAuthButton.jsx and GoogleAuthButton.jsx render social authentication buttons. Verify that each button has an accessible name that is both visible (text) and programmatically determinable. If either button is icon-only or relies solely on a logo SVG for its label, add aria-label describing the action (e.g., 'Sign in with GitHub', 'Sign in with Google'). Also ensure any SVG logos have aria-hidden='true'.
acceptanceCriteria: GithubAuthButton.jsx button has an accessible name describing the action
GoogleAuthButton.jsx button has an accessible name describing the action
SVG logos in both buttons have aria-hidden='true'
Build passes with no errors

userStories.title: Fix NotesEditor missing aria-label on save/discard toolbar buttons
userStories.description: NotesEditor.jsx renders a toolbar with save and discard buttons that appear when editing notes. If these buttons are icon-only (showing only icons like Save, X, or Check), they must have aria-label describing their action. Read NotesEditor.jsx and add aria-label to any icon-only action buttons, and aria-hidden='true' to icons inside labeled buttons.
acceptanceCriteria: NotesEditor.jsx icon-only toolbar buttons have descriptive aria-label attributes
Icons within buttons with labels have aria-hidden='true'
Build passes with no errors

userStories.title: Add aria-hidden to AuthLayout decorative visual elements
userStories.description: AuthLayout.jsx wraps login, register, forgot-password, and reset-password pages with a shared layout that may include decorative background shapes, brand illustrations, or pattern elements. Read AuthLayout.jsx and add aria-hidden='true' to any decorative SVG or div-based visual elements that convey no information to screen reader users.
acceptanceCriteria: AuthLayout.jsx decorative visual elements have aria-hidden='true' or equivalent
Meaningful structural elements (nav, main, headings) are not hidden
Build passes with no errors
